relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: After the prayer, Jesus enters the garden beyond Kidron.
Judas knows the meeting place of intimacy.
Lanterns, torches, and weapons enter the garden.
Jesus knows what is coming and steps forward.
They seek Jesus the Nazarene; he answers, I am.
The I am word throws them backward to the ground.
The question is asked again.
He gives himself and protects his own.
None of those given is lost.
Peter strikes with the sword and cuts Malchus’ ear.
Jesus refuses the sword and receives the Father’s cup.
They seize and bind him.
He is led first to Annas.
Caiaphas’ one-for-the-people counsel hovers over the trial.
Peter and another disciple follow into the high-priestly space.
Peter is brought through the door.
At the doorway, Peter says, I am not.
He warms himself by the charcoal fire.
Jesus is questioned about disciples and teaching.
He answers with public truth: nothing hidden.
The hearers can bear witness to his words.
A strike answers his truthful speech.
Jesus asks for witness to harm if harm was spoken.
Bound, he is sent to Caiaphas.
Peter’s second denial comes by the fire.
The garden witness presses him again.
The third denial meets the rooster.
Jesus is led to the Roman governor’s space at dawn.
Pilate asks for the accusation.
They assert harm without naming it clearly.
Authority and execution are pushed toward Rome.
The manner of death fulfills Jesus’ own word.
Pilate asks the kingship question.
Jesus questions the source of Pilate’s question.
Pilate distances himself from the Judean conflict.
Jesus’ reign is not sourced from this world-order.
His kingship is witness to truth; those from truth hear his voice.
Pilate asks, What is truth, while declaring no cause.
Passover release is offered around the king of the Judeans.
They choose Barabbas instead.
